headline: Master Prompt Engineering, Flash Fill Data Like a Pro, and Use Wrike to Run Consulting Projects

stories.title: Prompt Engineering Remains the Most In-Demand AI Skill in 2026
stories.summary: Prompt engineering—the ability to craft precise instructions for AI systems—is lowkey becoming essential across all industries. Companies are hiring specialists because literally everyone's using AI now, and quality matters.
stories.takeaway: Anyone can learn it without coding; it's all about asking the right questions and giving AI clear context to work with.

stories.title: Flash Fill: Excel's Hidden Gem That Saves Hours on Data Cleanup
stories.summary: Flash Fill is lowkey magic—give Excel two examples of what you want, hit Ctrl+E, and it automatically fills your entire column based on the pattern. No formulas needed, just pure AI-powered automation.
stories.takeaway: Perfect for splitting names, formatting phone numbers, extracting emails, or converting dates without writing a single formula.

stories.title: Wrike: Enterprise-Grade Project Management That Consultants Actually Love
stories.summary: Wrike's the go-to for management consultants managing multiple clients and complex projects. It's got customizable workflows, time tracking, real-time dashboards, and honestly, way more integrations than you'd think (400+).
stories.takeaway: Tbh it has a learning curve, but once you're set up, it brings total visibility across your entire portfolio and keeps everyone aligned.

watchNext.title: Why Prompt Engineering Might Not Be Your Most Valuable AI Skill Anymore
watchNext.why: As AI evolves, leadership and judgment skills are becoming way more critical than just writing prompts—worth thinking about for your career growth.

watchNext.title: AI's Growing Role in Management Consulting Workflows
watchNext.why: Over 70% of top consulting firms are already using AI-powered platforms—this shows the real shift happening in how consultants deliver value to clients.
